message:
perf(indexer): assemble staged lexical-rebuild shards by stitching tantivy index dirs instead of re-merging documents

When the lexical rebuild pipeline finishes the last stage, it has N
fully-built shard index directories that all share the same schema and
index settings. The previous finalizer,
`merge_lexical_rebuild_shard_index_tree`, re-ran a full Tantivy merge
pipeline over every doc across every shard — the same docs that were
already indexed, tokenized, stored, and written to disk moments
earlier. On large archives this second pass dominated the wall-clock
cost of a full lexical rebuild for no functional benefit: the output
index was byte-for-byte equivalent in content to a composition of the
inputs.

Add `TantivyIndex::assemble_compatible_index_directories(output, inputs)`
and wire `finalize_staged_lexical_rebuild_publish_artifact` to call it:

src/search/tantivy.rs
- New function opens each input index, ensures the tokenizer, loads
  metas, and composes a single output index by:
  * Copying every referenced managed file (segment files, delete
    files, store files, etc.) into the output dir once, deduped via a
    BTreeSet<PathBuf> so files referenced by multiple inputs aren't
    copied twice.
  * Merging `segments` vectors from each input into a single
    combined_segments list (no re-tokenization).
  * Taking the max `opstamp` across inputs as the combined opstamp so
    writer-generated segment ids stay monotonic after reopen.
  * Writing one `meta.json` with the unified schema + index_settings
    (these must match across inputs — the function errors out
    explicitly if they differ, instead of silently producing an
    invalid index).
- Rejects empty input slices up front with a clear error message.
- Relies on the existing `ensure_empty_merge_output_directory` helper
  to prevent assembling into a directory that still has old state.

src/indexer/mod.rs
- `finalize_staged_lexical_rebuild_publish_artifact` now calls
  `TantivyIndex::assemble_compatible_index_directories(output_path,
  input_paths)` and logs an INFO span
  "assembling final staged lexical rebuild generation from compatible
  artifacts without remerging docs" with the publish path and the
  number of staged artifacts being composed.
- `stage_root` and `max_parallel_jobs` are now unused by the fast
  path (assembly is O(files) I/O, not CPU-bound merge work) — kept
  in the signature so callers don't need to change, silenced with
  `let _ =`.
- The old CPU-bound merge path lives on as
  `merge_lexical_rebuild_shard_index_tree` behind
  `#[cfg_attr(not(test), allow(dead_code))]` so the old code is still
  reachable from tests that exercise the fallback directly, without
  triggering release-build unused-fn warnings.
  `LexicalRebuildShardMergeWork` is gated the same way.
- New unit test `finalize_staged_lexical_rebuild_publish_artifact_assembles_multiple_inputs_without_doc_remerge`
  builds a couple of fake shard packets (two distinct external ids,
  distinct message content), writes them out, runs the finalizer,
  and verifies the output index contains every expected doc and that
  schema/settings are preserved — i.e. the fast assembly path is
  behaviorally equivalent to the old merge path but does not
  re-tokenize docs.

Result: a full lexical rebuild on a realistic archive now spends its
finalization time on I/O (copying already-written segment files) plus
one JSON write, instead of on a second CPU-bound Tantivy merge pass.
